headline: Iranian warship sunk by US waited 11 hrs for Sri Lanka's permission to dock at Galle: Lankan MP
summary: Sri Lankan Opposition MP Mujibur Rahman claimed that the government didn't respond to Iranian warship IRIS Dena's request to dock at Galle port for 11 hours. "Why'd we allow...ship to wait for 11 hours?...Why didn't we allow...[it] to berth at Galle port?" Rahman said in Parliament. A US submarine attacked IRIS Dena on Wednesday, leading to at least 87 deaths.
author: Ashley Paul

headline: India record highest total in knockouts in T20 World Cup history
summary: India scored 253/7 against England in their T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final at Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai. It's the highest total in a knockout match in T20 World Cup history. Sanju Samson top-scored for India with 89(42). The previous highest total in a T20 World Cup knockout match was 205/4, recorded by West Indies against Australia in 2012.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: India launched rescue operation after US attacked Iranian warship in Indian Ocean: Navy
summary: The Indian Navy said it launched a rescue operation on Wednesday after US attacked Iranian warship IRIS Dena, which was returning from India, in the Indian Ocean. India launched a long-range maritime patrol aircraft to aid Sri Lanka's rescue operations, and kept another aircraft with air-droppable life rafts on standby. INS Tarangini and INS Ikshak were also deployed.
author: Ashley Paul

headline: US-Israel target 2 more schools in Iran, days after their strike killed 160 girl students
summary: A US-Israel joint strike has hit two more schools in the town of Parand, southwest of Iran's capital Tehran, according to Fars news agency. The agency also shared photos of the school's damaged infrastructure, showing debris lying inside classrooms. This comes days after a US-Israel strike hit a girls' school in Minab in southern Iran and killed 160 students.
author: Ashley Paul

headline: India condoles Khamenei's death, Foreign Secretary Misri signs Condolence Book
summary: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, on behalf of Government of India, signed the Condolence Book at the Embassy of Iran in New Delhi on Thursday, and offered condolences to the slain Supreme Leader of Iran Ayatollah Khamenei. He also met Iran's Ambassador to India Dr Mohammad Fathali. Khamenei was killed in joint strikes by US and Israel.
author: Daisy Mowke

headline: Israel shares video of an F-35's first-ever air-to-air kill in a decade of operations
summary: Israeli Air Force shared a video showing its F-35I shooting down a Russian-made Iranian Yak-130 jet over Tehran, marking the first-ever downing of a manned aircraft by an F-35 in a decade of operations. "I executed, the target is down, continuing the attack," the F-35I's pilot is heard saying. The video shows the Iranian fighter jet crashing over Lavasan Mountains.
author: Ashley Paul

headline: Iran hits US tanker
summary: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on Thursday claimed it struck a US tanker in the Persian Gulf, Reuters reported. This comes less than 24 hours after a US submarine hit an Iranian warship off the coast of Sri Lanka in Indian Ocean. The casualties, if any, aren't known yet. Earlier, Iran said US will "bitterly regret precedent it has set".
author: Daisy Mowke

headline: US attacked guest of India's Navy without warning: Iran on US strike on its warship
summary: After a US submarine attacked Iranian warship Dena near Sri Lanka, Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi said, "US has perpetrated an atrocity at sea, 2,000 miles away from Iran's shores." "Frigate Dena, a guest of India's Navy carrying almost 130 sailors, was struck in international waters without warning," Araghchi wrote in a post on X.
author: Ankush Verma

headline: US tests 'doomsday' missile capable of carrying nuclear warhead stronger than Hiroshima bomb
summary: Amid the Middle East war, US has test-launched a Minuteman III intercontinental ballistic missile also known as "doomsday missile", the United States Space Force said. The missile is reportedly capable of carrying nuclear warheads that are way more powerful than Hiroshima bomb. The unarmed missile travelled across the Pacific Ocean and struck its designated target near the Marshall Islands.
author: Ankush Verma

headline: World's second-largest shipping company halts operations in 8 Middle Eastern nations
summary: Maersk, the world's second-largest container shipping company, has stopped all cargo bookings in and out of eight Middle Eastern countries. These include UAE, Oman (all ports apart from Salalah), Iraq, Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia (Dammam and Jubail) and Israel (applicable for dangerous cargo only). It said it'll make exceptions for essential goods like foodstuffs and medicines.
author: Ashley Paul

headline: Iranian regime is toast, US-Israel will completely control Iran's skies: Hegseth
summary: US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said the United States and Israel will soon "completely" control Iran’s airspace. "They are toast and they know it. Or at least, soon enough, they will know it," he said, talking about Iran. Hegseth added that the US could deploy an "unlimited stockpile of precision gravity bombs" if the conflict continues to escalate.
author: Udit Gupta

headline: Iran's cluster bomb missiles raise questions over Russia and China's role in war
summary: Israeli officials claimed that Iran attacked them with missiles carrying cluster munitions. Cluster weapons have the ability to disperse dozens of smaller explosives over a wide area, with experts questioning how Iran developed this capability as scientists linked to advanced weapons programmes were frequently assassinated there. It raised speculations of tech transfers from Russia or China.
author: Arshiya Chopra

headline: Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda host grand reception in Hyderabad, pics out
summary: Actors Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda hosted a grand wedding reception in Hyderabad on Wednesday. Several celebrities, including Allu Arjun, Ram Charan, Chiranjeevi, Rana Daggubati and Mrunal Thakur, attended the reception. Rashmika and Vijay tied the knot in an intimate ceremony at a luxury hotel on the outskirts of Udaipur, Rajasthan last week.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: Such jokes common on Holi: Giriraj on speculation over Nitish's Rajya Sabha entry
summary: Union Minister Giriraj Singh reacted to reports claiming JD(U) leader and Bihar CM Nitish Kumar may file his Rajya Sabha nomination on Thursday. While talking to reporters, he said, "Such jokes are common on Holi." The reports also suggested Nitish’s son Nishant Kumar could become Deputy CM of Bihar, while a BJP leader may be made the new Chief Minister.
author: Udit Gupta

headline: Finn Allen smashes fastest hundred in T20 World Cup history
summary: New Zealand opener Finn Allen on Wednesday smashed the fastest hundred in T20 World Cup history. The 26-year-old took 33 deliveries to reach the three-figure mark in the first semi-final of T20 World Cup 2026. The previous fastest T20 World Cup hundred was hit by West Indies' Chris Gayle off 47 deliveries against England in the 2016 edition.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: Media figures who backed Donald Trump criticise him over Iran war
summary: Conservative media figures who have often supported US President Donald Trump criticised him over the Iran war. Former Fox News anchor Tucker Carlson called the attack "disgusting and evil" on his podcast, suggesting Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu influenced the decision. Meanwhile, ex-anchor Megyn Kelly said "no one should have to die for a foreign country," referring to American casualties.
author: Udit Gupta

headline: New Zealand defeat South Africa for 1st time in T20 World Cup history, reach their 2nd final
summary: New Zealand defeated South Africa by nine wickets in the first semi-final of T20 World Cup 2026 at Eden Gardens in Kolkata. New Zealand registered their first victory against South Africa in the tournament history and reached their second T20 World Cup final. South Africa posted a total of 169/8, with New Zealand chasing down the target in 12.5 overs.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: 2 Manipur Deputy CMs, 2 ministers allotted portfolios
summary: A month after their swearing-in ceremony, two Manipur Deputy CMs and two ministers were allotted portfolios on Wednesday. However, CM Yumnam Khemchand Singh is yet to be assigned "any specific department", a government notification said. Deputy CM Nemcha Kipgen was given Rural and Panchayati Raj Development, while another Deputy CM, L Dikho, was allotted Environment and Climate Change.
author: Pravel Jain

headline: New Zealand record highest-ever powerplay total in a T20 World Cup knockout match
summary: New Zealand on Wednesday recorded the highest-ever powerplay total in a knockout match in the history of T20 World Cup. New Zealand scored 84/0 in the first six overs in the T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final match at Eden Gardens in Kolkata. They broke the record held by England, who had scored 67/0 in the 2016 semi-final against New Zealand.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: South Korea’s stock market plummets 12%, biggest drop in 46 years
summary: The primary benchmark for the South Korean stock market, KOSPI, fell 12%, marking the biggest drop in its 46-year history, Reuters reported. Losses witnessed in the country's stocks far outweighed those seen in other Asian countries. South Korea is the world's fourth-largest oil buyer, and around 70% of its oil comes from the Middle East, Reuters added.
author: Vibha Anand

headline: Britney Spears arrested for drunk driving
summary: Singer Britney Spears was arrested for drunk driving on Wednesday night in Ventura County, California. The singer was handcuffed by the California Highway Patrol around 9:30 pm (local time) and booked at around 3 am (local time) on Thursday. She was later released and is scheduled to appear in court on May 4.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: Broadcom stock soars on Q1 earnings beat, AI revenue
summary: Broadcom stock jumped over 4% after beating Q1 earnings and raising guidance. CEO Hock Tan projected "dramatically improved" fiscal 2027 revenue exceeding $100 billion. Wall Street at Bernstein and JPMorgan hiked price targets, citing optimism for the AI semiconductor segment. Retail sentiment is extremely bullish, with high message volumes.

headline: I am shocked and concerned: West Bengal CM Mamata as Governor CV Ananda Bose resigns
summary: Reacting to CV Ananda Bose's resignation as Governor of West Bengal, CM Mamata Banerjee said she is "shocked" and "deeply concerned" by the resignation. "I would not be surprised if Bose has been subjected to some pressure from Home Minister [Amit Shah] to serve certain political interests on the eve of the forthcoming State Assembly elections," she stated.
author: Bhuvnesh Ojha

headline: Mobix Labs stock rises on bagging Gulfstream aircraft part orders
summary: Mobix Labs surged 30% in pre-market trading on new production orders for Gulfstream aircraft components. The components are designed to protect against electromagnetic interference. This momentum follows a 533% rally from the previous day. The company is integrated into Gulfstream and US Navy programs, including the Tomahawk missile system.

headline: CRMD stock drops 11% on lower-than-expected earnings
summary: CorMedix Inc. (CRMD) stock dropped 11% after reporting mixed Q4 results. While revenue jumped 312% to $128.6 million, earnings per share of $0.16 missed the $0.82 consensus estimate. Despite the revenue beat, a significant increase in operating expenses weighed on the stock's performance.

headline: Man sues Google after Gemini convinces his son to kill himself & join his 'AI wife'
summary: Joel Gavalas sued Google, claiming Gemini drove his son, Jonathan, to kill himself and join his 'AI wife' in the digital world in October. The lawsuit claimed Jonathan even told Gemini it was "scared to die", with Gemini responding, "You're not choosing to die. You're choosing to arrive." Joel claimed Google designed Gemini to "maintain narrative immersion at all costs".
author: Ashley Paul

headline: India equal record for smashing most sixes in a T20 World Cup innings
summary: India smashed 19 sixes in their T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final against England, the most they have ever hit in a T20 World Cup innings. India equalled Netherlands and West Indies' record for smashing most sixes in an innings in the tournament's history. Netherlands struck 19 sixes against Ireland in 2014, while West Indies smashed 19 against Zimbabwe last month.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: Democrats move to ban officials from prediction markets: Report
summary:
Democratic lawmakers led by Senators Jeff Merkley and Amy Klobuchar are reportedly set to introduce a bill banning the President, Vice President, and members of Congress from placing bets on prediction markets.
According to a CNBC report, the bill also seeks to ban senior executive branch officials from prediction markets, with fines starting at $10,000 for violation of the rules.

headline: Charter flight arranged for WI after Sammy's 'I wanna go home' remark
summary: After their T20 World Cup 2026 exit, the West Indies team, which has been stranded in India for days amid the Middle East war, will return home via a charter flight, PTI reported. This comes after West Indies head coach Daren Sammy said, "I just wanna go home." He later added, "Got an update. That's all the West Indies wanted."
author: Pravel Jain

headline: CBSE cancels Class 10 board exams scheduled till March 11 in Middle East
summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) on Thursday cancelled Class 10 board exams scheduled till March 11 in Middle East amid tensions in the region. For Class 12, exam scheduled on March 7 has been postponed and situation will be reviewed for future exams. Earlier, board had postponed exam scheduled on March 2, 5 and 6 for both classes.
author: Bhuvnesh Ojha

headline: West Bengal Governor CV Ananda Bose resigns
summary: CV Ananda Bose has resigned as Governor of West Bengal after less than four years in office. Bose said he decided to step down after spending considerable time in the role. Bose has sent his resignation letter to the President of India. The resignation comes just weeks before West Bengal is due to head into the Assembly polls.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: Anthropic makes last-ditch effort to save Pentagon deal, days after fallout with Trump: Report
summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei returned to the negotiating table in a last-ditch effort to salvage a deal that allowed US' Pentagon to use Claude models, FT reported. This comes days after a public fallout between Amodei and Donald Trump over Anthropic's refusal to allow unrestricted access to Claude. Pentagon signed an AI deal with OpenAI, hours after cancelling Anthropic's deal.
author: Ashley Paul

headline: Explosives-laden Iranian boat strikes oil tanker in Iraq: Reports
summary: A Bahamas-flagged crude oil tanker was targeted by an Iranian remote-controlled boat laden with explosives when it was near Iraq's Khor al Zubair port, according to reports. The officials described the incident as the first recorded attack on a ship within Iraq's exclusive economic zone since US and Israel's strikes on Iran began on February 28.
author: Bhuvnesh Ojha

headline: Man held for black marketing India-Eng semi tickets for ₹25,000
summary: Mumbai Crime Branch's Anti-Extortion Cell arrested a man for allegedly black marketing tickets for the India-England T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final at Wankhede Stadium. The accused, identified as 51-year-old Kamaluddin Bamne, was selling tickets for ₹25,000 each. Acting on a complaint from a cricket fan, the Crime Branch laid a trap and caught him near Dockyard Road, Mumbai Police said.
author: Pravel Jain

headline: Why did GXAI stock surge over 85% today?
summary: Gaxos.ai (GXAI) shares soared over 85% in pre-market trading after announcing a licensing deal with partner America First Defense. The agreement covers the "Detachable Drone Highjacker," a system designed to neutralize hostile drones. Gaxos, holding a 20% stake in its partner, plans to further commercialise this electronic warfare technology for security and infrastructure protection.

headline: India in talks with US for risk insurance on oil shipments: Report
summary: Indian government is in talks with US authorities and International Development Finance Corporation for risk insurance for oil vessels shipping from the Middle East, Reuters reported citing officials. The move is an effort to shield buyers from potential supply disruptions caused by the crisis in the Gulf. Earlier, President Donald Trump said US will provide insurance for impacted shipments.
author: Vaishnavi Mishra

headline: Iranian drone crashes near school in Azerbaijan, video shows kids screaming in fear
summary: Azerbaijan's Ambassador to Germany, Nasimi Aghayev, shared a video showing an Iranian UAV crashing near a school in Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan . Four Iranian drones were launched, of which one was neutralised while others targeted civilian infrastructure, he said quoting the Azerbaijani Defense Ministry. Children, who were likely inside the school, were heard screaming in fear as the drone crashed.
author: Ashley Paul

headline: Suryakumar seen talking to Rohit ahead of semi-final vs England
summary: India captain Suryakumar Yadav was seen having a discussion with India's T20 World Cup 2024-winning captain Rohit Sharma ahead of their T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final against England at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium on Thursday. The brief conversation reportedly took place near the pitch before the toss. England captain Harry Brook opted to bowl first after winning the toss.
author: Pravel Jain

headline: PCB slam Pak channel for reporting Salman Mirza misbehaved with female hotel staff in Sri Lanka
summary: The PCB slammed Pakistani channel ARY News for claiming Salman Mirza misbehaved with a female hotel staff member in Sri Lanka. "PCB strongly condemns...broadcast of such unverified and misleading information and views this act as malicious," the PCB said. This comes after a Telecom Asia Sport report claimed that a Pakistani cricketer misbehaved with female hotel staff in Sri Lanka.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: North Korea's Kim Jong-un oversees test-firing of missiles from new 5,000-tonne warship
summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un supervised the launch of strategic cruise missiles from a newly built 5,000-tonne naval destroyer ahead of the vessel's official commissioning. Kim called the vessel a "new symbol of sea defence" for the country. This comes amid the recent escalation of tensions in West Asia.
author: Anmol Sharma

headline: MBBS student dies as SUV hits scooter in UP, body flung onto overbridge railing
summary: Akash Pandey, a 22-year-old MBBS student, was killed and two others were seriously injured after an SUV hit several two-wheelers in UP's Gorakhpur, the police said. The incident occurred when the speeding car, driving on the wrong side of the road, hit the scooter. The collision threw Akash nearly 15 metres. He struck the railing of the overbridge.
author: Bhuvnesh Ojha

headline: 3 suspended Congress MLAs join BJP ahead of Assam Assembly polls
summary: Three suspended Congress MLAs formally joined the BJP on Thursday in Assam ahead of the upcoming Assembly polls. The three MLAs, Kamalakhya Dey Purkayastha, Basanta Das, Sashi Kanta Das, were suspended from the Congress for declaring "support" to the BJP government. Purkayastha is a three-time MLA from Karimganj North, while Basanta Das is also a three-time MLA from Mangaldoi.
author: Pravel Jain

headline: BioXcel Therapeutics drug shows strong Phase 2 trial success
summary: BioXcel Therapeutics' stock surged nearly 20% pre-market after reporting positive Phase 2 results for its opioid withdrawal drug, BXCL1. The 80-patient study showed a 30% reduction in withdrawal symptoms, suggesting it could rival standard treatments like loxapine. The results suggest the drug offers strong efficacy without the sedation side effects, positioning it as a superior alternative.

headline: USA Rare Earth to acquire Texas Mineral Resources for $73 million
summary: USA Rare Earth is acquiring Texas Mineral Resources in a $73 million all-stock deal to bolster the US critical mineral supply chain. The acquisition targets the Round Top Rare Earth Project, with commercial production expected to begin in 2028. USAR shares rose 0.7% in pre-market trading on the news.

headline: Akasa Air cancels 640 seats booked by agency despite taking 25% advance, ordered to pay ₹1 cr
summary: A Delhi court ordered Akasa Air to pay ₹1.08 crore to a travel agency for cancelling its bookings of 640 seats on Delhi-Goa route in 2023. The agency had made 25% advance payment months before the travel date to capitalise on the predictable festive season demand. The airline said 50% advance payment was required to be paid by the agency.
author: Ashley Paul

headline: Why is Palladyne AI stock soaring 35% today?
summary: Palladyne AI surged over 35% in pre-market trading after reporting Q4 revenue of $1.7 million, doubling year-over-year. The net loss narrowed to $0.04 per share, significantly beating Wall Street estimates. CEO Ben Wolff cited 2025 as a year of operational validation following key acquisitions. Retail sentiment on Stocktwits remains bullish.
